blocks.md: A comparison gives 1 or 0. To *choose between two values* on a condition, the cleanest idiom is **pick**: index a two-item list with `1 + condition`. When the condition is 0 you get the first item, when it is 1 you get the second.
The other idiom is plain arithmetic: multiply by the mask. `⍵ × ⍵ > 0` keeps positives and zeroes everything else — a one-line clamp, no branch in sight.
`∊` (membership) asks *is each of these in that set?* — and it is the backbone of selection. `'hangman' ∊ 'an'` marks every letter of the word that has been guessed.

blocks.prompt: Define sign so that a positive ⍵ gives 'pos' and anything else 'neg', then evaluate sign 5. Use the pick idiom. Expect pos.
Using a mask (no ⌈), turn the vector ¯3 4 ¯1 6 ¯2 into 0 4 0 6 0 — keep positives, zero the rest.
blocks.hint: multiply each element by whether it is greater than 0

blocks.prompt: Mark which letters of 'banana' appear in the guessed set 'an' (1 for guessed, 0 for not).
Of the numbers 1 2 3 4 5 6, mark which are members of the set 2 3 5 (the primes below 6).
blocks.hint: left ∊ right tests each left element for membership in right
